segments.text: station full of people trying to go somewhere. Amongst the hustle and bustle
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: two large cases rattle at the top of two trolleys. They're being pushed by
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: two boys, James Potter and Albus Potter. Their mother, Ginny, follows after. A
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: 37 year old man, Harry, and his daughter Lily on the shoulders. Dad, he
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: keeps saying it. James, give it a rest. I've only said he might be
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: Slytherin. And he might sew. Fine. You'll write to me, won't you? Every day if you
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: want us to. No, not every day. James says most people only get letters from home
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: about once a month. I don't want to... Well, we wrote to your brother three
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: times a week last year. What? James? Yes, you may not want to believe
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: everything he tells you about Hogwarts. He likes a laugh, your brother.
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: Can I go now, please? All you have to do is walk straight into that wall
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: between platforms. Nine and ten. I'm so excited. Don't stop. Don't be scared.
segments.speaker: Unknown 1

segments.text: You'll crash into it. That's very important.
segments.speaker: Unknown 1
